levels.2: Outcome is vague, abstract, or expressed as a broad aspiration.
levels.3: Some clarity, but interpretation varies across readers.
levels.4: Outcome is mostly clear, with minimal room for interpretation.
levels.5: Outcome is explicit, concrete, and unambiguous; anyone can restate it accurately.

levels.2: Purpose is generic or disconnected from the real mission.
levels.3: Purpose is implied but not explicit.
levels.4: Purpose is stated and mostly clear, though not inspiring.
levels.5: Purpose compellingly explains why the outcome matters and connects to the larger mission.

dimensions.name: Alignment with Higher Direction, Strategy & Business Context

levels.1: Contradicts wider direction or ignores clear strategic realities.
levels.2: Several conflicts or unclear alignment with strategy, values, or context.
levels.3: Partially aligned; some tensions or vagueness.
levels.4: Mostly aligned with strategy and context, with minor gaps to resolve.
levels.5: Fully consistent with organisational strategy, current priorities, and external/internal business context. No contradictions.

levels.1: 5MAP tasks do not relate to the intent or contradict it.
levels.2: Tasks are largely procedural and weakly linked to intent outcomes.
levels.3: Some tasks relate well; others are weakly linked or ambiguous.
levels.4: Most tasks are aligned and provide reasonable line-of-sight to intent.
levels.5: Downstream tasks clearly express how the team will advance the intent; strong line-of-sight from intent to priorities to tasks.

levels.1: Very long, repetitive, or filled with extraneous information.
levels.2: Overlong or rambling; difficult to remember.
levels.3: Contains unnecessary detail but still readable.
levels.4: Short but slightly wordy; still memorable.
levels.5: Very concise (1 to 3 sentences); easy to recall and repeat.

levels.3: Mix of outcomes and tasks; ambiguous emphasis.
levels.4: Mostly outcomes with minor task-like phrasing.
levels.5: States a clear end-state with no tasks or instructions.

levels.1: Provides no help for empowerment; requires constant oversight.
levels.2: Too vague or too prescriptive for decentralisation.
levels.3: Offers some direction but is insufficient for initiative-taking.
levels.4: Generally helpful but may require occasional clarification.
levels.5: Teams could make most decisions independently using this intent alone.

levels.1: No practical way to verify relevance or progress.
levels.4: Mostly testable, though some room for doubt.
levels.5: Clear enough that people can judge whether a proposed action supports the intent.
Inspiring, energising, and feels worth striving for.

interpretation_bands.label: Exceptional intent, ready for operational use.
Strong; may benefit from light refinement.
